findings.justification: Profile links and user status values are concatenated into an HTML string and dispatched to admin emails without escaping, so a malicious username or status value could inject arbitrary HTML into administrator email notifications.
The public send_notification method performs no capability or nonce check before dispatching email to all administrators, making it callable by any code path without access control validation.

When revoking caps for an actively logged-in suspicious user the account is only set to 'inactive' rather than 'rejected', and the current session is never invalidated, allowing the attacker's existing session to remain usable.

Raw POST form data from UM()->form()->post_form is stored directly in user meta under um_user_blocked__metadata without sanitization, which could expose sensitive field values or inject malicious content if that meta is later rendered in an admin UI.

Although the HTTP_USER_AGENT is sanitized with sanitize_text_field before being stored in user meta, it is only sanitized for display, not for the stored metadata array that may be read back and rendered in admin screens without further escaping.
